name: Adia Nutrition, Inc.
business: ADIA Nutrition, Inc. sells biologic products derived from umbilical cord stem cells for therapeutic use, including Autologous Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation (AHSCT), as well as dietary supplements. The company operates Adia Med clinics that administer stem cell treatments and Adia Labs that supplies biologic products to third-party clinics and internal use. Revenue comes from medical procedures, sales of biologics, and supplement sales.

exposures.sentence: ADIA is dedicated to revolutionizing healthcare through innovative partnerships. The primary focus is to work closely with healthcare providers and health insurance companies to facilitate and provide Autologous Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation (AHSCT).

Our UCB-SC contain at least 100 million stem cells and 3 trillion exosomes per unit, and are registered with the FDA under section 361 of the Public Health Service Act (PHSA), which provides for the registration of human cells, tissues and cellular and tissue-based products (HCT/Ps) to be registered pursuant to compliance with FDA 21 CFR part 1271.
We sell our supplements on a direct-to-consumer basis (i.e., www.cementfactory.co) as well as through other direct to consumer channels/portals.

tamClaims.claim: The stem cell therapy sector is projected to reach $28.89 billion globally by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: The stem cell therapy sector is a rapidly expanding segment of biotechnology and regenerative medicine, projected to reach $28.89 billion globally by 2030 according to Grand View Research.

tamClaims.claim: The overall HSCT market is valued at around USD 3.74 billion in 2025, projected to reach USD 6.25 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: As of 2025, the overall HSCT market is valued at around USD 3.74 billion, projected to reach USD 6.25 billion by 2030 at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 10.79%.

tamClaims.claim: Longer-term HSCT market forecasts surpass USD 10.16 billion by 2037.
tamClaims.sentence: Longer-term forecasts indicate even stronger growth, with the market surpassing USD 10.16 billion by 2037 a CAGR of 9.7% from 2024.

tamClaims.claim: The umbilical cord stem cell market has a global valuation around USD 7-8 billion in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: Market data varies due to differences in scope (e.g., banking services vs. therapeutic applications), but consensus points to a global valuation around USD 7-8 billion in 2025, with strong expansion projected through 2032 and beyond.
